mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS at 0.08, an always-on low-rate signal predicts positive 20-day S&P 500 returns because near-zero policy rates reduce equity discount rates and support risk-taking relative to cash.
regime_hint: monetary easing or zero-rate policy regime
failure_condition: Reject if periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.25 do not deliver higher average 20-day S&P 500 returns than periods with FEDFUNDS above 0.25, or if the return spread is negative in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration above its recent range predicts negative 20-day S&P 500 returns because higher inflation raises expected policy tightening and compresses equity valuation multiples.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized change crosses above its trailing 36-month 75th percentile
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest snapshot value is 270.981 while FEDFUNDS is 0.08
regime_hint: inflation scare during highly accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: Reject if triggered CPIAUCSL acceleration episodes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 60% of the time, or if the conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE combined with weak PAYEMS growth predicts negative 60-day S&P 500 returns because labor-market deterioration reduces household income growth and raises recession-risk premia.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months and PAYEMS 3-month change is less than or equal to 0
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest snapshot value is 5.9 and PAYEMS latest snapshot value is 145759.0
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger has nonnegative average 60-day S&P 500 forward returns in at least 70% of walk-forward folds, or if UNRATE-PAYEMS interaction terms add no predictive power versus single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Positive RSAFS growth together with falling UNRATE predicts positive 60-day S&P 500 returns because strengthening retail demand and improving labor income lift expected corporate revenues.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change crosses above 0 while UNRATE 3-month change is below 0
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest snapshot value is 620214.0 and UNRATE latest snapshot value is 5.9
failure_condition: Reject if triggered demand-labor improvement episodes do not produce higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than nontriggered months, or if the conditional win rate is below 50% out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: An always-on positive INDPRO level signal predicts positive 60-day S&P 500 returns when production is near or above the 2017 base, because operating leverage converts stable output into higher earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO values at or above 100 are followed by lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than INDPRO values below 100 in walk-forward testing, or if the sign of the coefficient is persistently negative.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST predicts negative 60-day S&P 500 returns because weakening residential construction reduces cyclically sensitive activity, credit demand, and durable-goods spillovers.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST drawdown triggers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if triggered returns are not below unconditional 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: FEDFUNDS below 0.25 combined with UNRATE above 5.5 predicts positive 20-day S&P 500 returns because investors price policy support as dominant over still-elevated labor slack during recovery.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is below 0.25 and UNRATE is above 5.5
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value is 0.08 and UNRATE latest snapshot value is 5.9
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-rate high-unemployment regime has lower 20-day S&P 500 returns than other regimes, or if the interaction coefficient is not positive in rolling out-of-sample estimates.

mechanism_narrative: CPIAUCSL acceleration combined with falling INDPRO predicts negative 5-day S&P 500 returns because markets rapidly reprice stagflation risk through lower real growth and higher discount-rate expectations.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change crosses above its trailing 24-month median and INDPRO 3-month change crosses below 0
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest snapshot value is 270.981 and INDPRO latest snapshot value is 100.0951
failure_condition: Reject if triggered CPIAUCSL-INDPRO stagflation episodes generate nonnegative average 5-day S&P 500 returns, or if the joint trigger performs no worse than either single proxy alone.
